login.title: Sign in
login.subtitle: Connect to your IMAP mailbox
login.hint: Credentials are sent to your self-hosted API over a secure session.
login.tagline: Light mail that stays out of the way.

settings.title: Settings
settings.themeHint: Paper Quiet supports light and dark
settings.moodHint: Applies to login and the whole mail client

mail.emptySelectionHint: Choose a conversation from the list to read it
mail.sentSpamHint: If the recipient does not see it, ask them to check Spam — deliverability depends on SPF/DKIM/DMARC on your mail server.
mail.emptySentHint: Messages you send will show up here
mail.emptyDraftsHint: Save a draft from compose to continue later
mail.emptySpamHint: Messages filtered as junk appear here. Check this folder if a reply seems missing.
mail.offline: No connection — showing last loaded mail
mail.imagesBlocked: Remote images are blocked to protect privacy

errors.unauthorized: Session expired — please sign in again
errors.notImplemented: Not connected to the API yet

codes.mail.session_invalid: Session expired — please sign in again
codes.mail.impersonate_invalid: Admin login link expired or invalid
codes.mail.impersonate_unavailable: Superuser login is not configured on the server

compose.title: New message
compose.subject: Subject
compose.filesTooLarge: Attachments exceed the 25 MB total limit

mailport.title: Embed mail into your product
mailport.body: Mailport is the lightweight embed surface for Wernanmail — same stack, scoped for host apps.
mailport.step2: Pass theme/compact query params to match the host UI.
mailport.step3: Later: scoped API tokens so the iframe can open a real mailbox without a full login form.

landing.headline: Mail that stays out of the way.
landing.lede: Self-hosted SMTP, IMAP, webmail, and admin — built to live in under half a gigabyte of RAM.

why.title: A whole mail stack that still leaves room to breathe.
why.body: No Mailcow-class footprint. Pure Go daemons, SQLite, Maildir, antispam, and a quiet UI — made for 1–2 GB hosts that still deserve real corporate mail.
why.meter: Target: under 500 MB while the stack is running.

showcase.title: Not a mockup — the real UI.
showcase.body: Screens from the repo: admin console, sign-in, compose, and mobile moods — same stack you install.
showcase.label: Product screenshots
showcase.repo: All shots in docs/mockups on GitHub →

alt.inbox: Wernanmail admin overview with deliverability and DNS chips
alt.compose: Compose dialog in the webmail client

caption.inbox: Operator console — domains, queue, deliverability, under ~100 MB on a live host.
caption.signin: Aurora hero and mood dots carry into the inbox.
caption.compose: HTML compose, attachments, drafts — without a heavy editor.

moods.title: Five moods. One calm inbox.
moods.body: Harbor at dawn, reef through the afternoon, ember after dark. Pick a mood — the brand site and the mail client shift together.

install.title: One command on a clean Linux host.
install.body: Docker builds the stack, bootstraps secrets, and brings up SMTP, IMAP, API, webmail, and admin. Then point DNS and TLS at wernanmail.ru.
